Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the determinant of each square matrix.
Question: $$\left [\begin{matrix}6 -2 \\ 3 -1\end{matrix}\right ]$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the determinant of each square matrix.
Question: $$\left [\begin{matrix}-4 8 \\ -3 5\end{matrix}\right ]$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the determinant of each square matrix.
Question: $$\left [\begin{matrix}-3 &  &  & 5 \\ 0 &  &  & -4\end{matrix}\right ]$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the determinant of each square matrix.
Question: $$\left [\begin{matrix}-2 &  &  & 0 \\ 7 &  &  & -5\end{matrix}\right ]$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find and then evaluate the indicated minors.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}3 &  &  & -1 &  &  & 4 \\ -1 &  &  & 0 &  &  & -2 \\ -4 &  &  & 1 &  &  & 5\end{matrix}\right |$$ Find the minor ⓐ $$a_{1}$$ ⓑ $$b_{2}$$ ⓒ $$c_{3}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find and then evaluate the indicated minors.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}-1 &  &  & -3 &  &  & 2 \\ 4 &  &  & -2 &  &  & -1 \\ -2 &  &  & 0 &  &  & -3\end{matrix}\right |$$ Find the minor ⓐ $$a_{1}$$ ⓑ $$b_{1}$$ ⓒ $$c_{2}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find and then evaluate the indicated minors.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}2 &  &  & -3 &  &  & -4 \\ -1 &  &  & 2 &  &  & -3 \\ 0 &  &  & -1 &  &  & -2\end{matrix}\right |$$ Find the minor ⓐ $$a_{2}$$ ⓑ $$b_{2}$$ ⓒ $$c_{2}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, find and then evaluate the indicated minors.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}-2 &  &  & -2 &  &  & 3 \\ 1 &  &  & -3 &  &  & 0 \\ -2 &  &  & 3 &  &  & -2\end{matrix}\right |$$ Find the minor ⓐ $$a_{3}$$ ⓑ $$b_{3}$$ ⓒ $$c_{3}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate each determinant by expanding by minors along the first row.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}-2 &  &  & 3 &  &  & -1 \\ -1 &  &  & 2 &  &  & -2 \\ 3 &  &  & 1 &  &  & -3\end{matrix}\right |$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate each determinant by expanding by minors along the first row.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}4 &  &  & -1 &  &  & -2 \\ -3 &  &  & -2 &  &  & 1 \\ -2 &  &  & -5 &  &  & 7\end{matrix}\right |$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate each determinant by expanding by minors along the first row.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}-2 &  &  & -3 &  &  & -4 \\ 5 &  &  & -6 &  &  & 7 \\ -1 &  &  & 2 &  &  & 0\end{matrix}\right |$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate each determinant by expanding by minors along the first row.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}1 &  &  & 3 &  &  & -2 \\ 5 &  &  & -6 &  &  & 4 \\ 0 &  &  & -2 &  &  & -1\end{matrix}\right |$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate each determinant by expanding by minors.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}-5 &  &  & -1 &  &  & -4 \\ 4 &  &  & 0 &  &  & -3 \\ 2 &  &  & -2 &  &  & 6\end{matrix}\right |$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate each determinant by expanding by minors.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}4 &  &  & -1 &  &  & 3 \\ 3 &  &  & -2 &  &  & 2 \\ -1 &  &  & 0 &  &  & 4\end{matrix}\right |$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate each determinant by expanding by minors.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}3 &  &  & 5 &  &  & 4 \\ -1 &  &  & 3 &  &  & 0 \\ -2 &  &  & 6 &  &  & 1\end{matrix}\right |$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate each determinant by expanding by minors.
Question: $$\left |\begin{matrix}2 &  &  & -4 &  &  & -3 \\ 5 &  &  & -1 &  &  & -4 \\ 3 &  &  & 2 &  &  & 0\end{matrix}\right |$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}-2 x + 3 y = 3 \\ x + 3 y = 12\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 2 y = -5 \\ 2 x - 3 y = -4\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 3 y = -9 \\ 2 x + 5 y = 4\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x + y = -4 \\ 3 x - 2 y = -6\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 2 y = -5 \\ 2 x - 3 y = -4\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 3 y = -9 \\ 2 x + 5 y = 4\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}5 x - 3 y = -1 \\ 2 x - y = 2\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x + 8 y = -3 \\ 2 x + 5 y = -3\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}6 x - 5 y + 2 z = 3 \\ 2 x + y - 4 z = 5 \\ 3 x - 3 y + z = -1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}4 x - 3 y + z = 7 \\ 2 x - 5 y - 4 z = 3 \\ 3 x - 2 y - 2 z = -7\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x - 5 y + 3 z = 8 \\ 3 x - y + 4 z = 7 \\ x + 3 y + 2 z = -3\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}11 x + 9 y + 2 z = -9 \\ 7 x + 5 y + 3 z = -7 \\ 4 x + 3 y + z = -3\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x + 2 z = 0 \\ 4 y + 3 z = -2 \\ 2 x - 5 y = 3\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x + 5 y = 4 \\ 3 y - z = 3 \\ 4 x + 3 z = -3\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 y + 3 z = -1 \\ 5 x + 3 y = -6 \\ 7 x + z = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x - z = -3 \\ 5 y + 2 z = -6 \\ 4 x + 3 y = -8\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x + y = 3 \\ 6 x + 3 y = 9\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 4 y = -1 \\ -3 x + 12 y = 3\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}-3 x - y = 4 \\ 6 x + 2 y = -16\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}4 x + 3 y = 2 \\ 20 x + 15 y = 5\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x + y - 3 z = -1 \\ y - z = 0 \\ \text{unicode[:-]} x + 2 y = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x + 3 y + z = 12 \\ x + y + z = 9 \\ 3 x + 4 y + 2 z = 20\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x + 4 y - 3 z = -2 \\ 2 x + 3 y - z = -12 \\ x + y - 2 z = 6\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve each system of equations using Cramer’s Rule.
Question: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 2 y + 3 z = 1 \\ x + y - 3 z = 7 \\ 3 x - 4 y + 5 z = 7\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, determine whether the given points are collinear.
Question: $$( 0 , 1 ) ,$$ $$( 2 , 0 ) ,$$ and $$( -2 , 2 ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, determine whether the given points are collinear.
Question: $$( 0 , -5 ) ,$$ $$( -2 , -2 ) ,$$ and $$( 2 , -8 ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, determine whether the given points are collinear.
Question: $$( 4 , -3 ) ,$$ $$( 6 , -4 ) ,$$ and $$( 2 , -2 ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, determine whether the given points are collinear.
Question: $$( -2 , 1 ) ,$$ $$( -4 , 4 ) ,$$ and $$( 0 , -2 ) .$$
Explain the difference between a square matrix and its determinant. Give an example of each.
Explain what is meant by the minor of an entry in a square matrix.
Explain how to decide which row or column you will use to expand a $$3 \times 3$$ determinant.
Explain the steps for solving a system of equations using Cramer’s rule.
